business.headline: Warner Bros investors approve Paramount's $111-billion takeoverShareholders of Warner Bros Discovery have approved its proposed merger with Paramount Global, clearing a major hurdle for the USD-111 billion deal. The transaction, including debt, would create a media giant and significantly reshape the Hollywood entertainment landscape.

Using UPI daily? Here's why your monthly budget feels tighterFrom groceries to coffee, UPI is now part of daily life. But while each payment feels minor, the total can add up faster than expected, leaving many wondering where their money really went.

Between records and reality: What a special inspection revealed about labour law gapsIn the fourth episode in this series on labour laws and Inspector Raj, this article examines how a special inspection exposed the gap between official compliance records, weak enforcement and workplace reality.

Are you using your no claim bonus smartly or losing money?By treating a no-claim bonus as a strategic asset rather than a simple discount, you can protect your savings and ensure that your insurance remains both affordable and comprehensive.

Sensex sinks 852 points, Nifty slips below 24,200; Dr Reddy's jumps nearly 9%The BSE Sensex closed at 77,664.00, down 852.49 points or 1.09 per cent. The NSE Nifty 50 settled at 24,173.05, lower by 205.05 points or 0.84 per cent, slipping below the 24,200 mark.

Bikaji founder Shiv Ratan Agarwal passes away at 75Shiv Ratan Agarwal, founder of Bikaji Foods, died at 75 in Chennai, leaving behind a legacy of transforming a regional snack business into a globally recognised brand.

Thought UAE free zones meant 0% corporate tax? Here's the realityThe idea of zero corporate tax has made the UAE a popular choice for many investors. But the promise of 0% tax is not as straightforward as it seems, and comes with conditions many often overlook.

Tax clearance for foreign travel: Do you need it every time you leave India?Section 420 of the Income-tax Act, 2025 deals with tax clearance requirements for certain individuals leaving India. While the provision exists in the new law, it is not intended to apply universally.

Just 1-2% traders drive 70% India's F&O volumes: Nithin Kamath warns of imbalanceIndia's booming derivatives market may look large from the outside, but the reality is far more concentrated. Zerodha's Nithin Kamath says a tiny group of traders is driving most of the activity, raising concerns about balance.

Crude oil above $100 again: Will petrol, diesel get costlier soon?Global crude oil prices climbed above $100 per barrel on Thursday, raising a question millions of Indian consumers care about most. Will this lead to costlier petrol and diesel prices?

Rupee slides to 94 against US dollar: Is crude oil the biggest risk right now?The rupee has already fallen nearly 1% this week and has slipped well past its recent highs of around 92.50. That earlier rise was largely supported by measures taken by the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) in late March and April.

Dr Reddy's shares jump 10% as Sensex falls: Why is the pharma stock rising?Shares of Dr Reddy's Laboratories surged sharply on Thursday, extending gains to 10% even as Dalal Street remained under pressure amid rising crude oil prices and global uncertainty.

Sensex tumbles 800 points: Why is the stock market falling?Dalal Street fell sharply on Thursday as crude oil surged above $100, banking and auto stocks declined, and investors worried about the economic fallout from the West Asia conflict.
Sensex, Nifty sink as crude oil prices reach $100; IndiGo down 2%The S&P BSE Sensex was down by 625.09 points to 77,891.40, while the NSE Nifty50 lost 173.90 points to 24,204.20 as of 9:26 am.

Stock market today: Will rising crude and Iran tensions drag Sensex, Nifty down?Early indicators suggest a weak start. GIFT Nifty futures were trading at 24,138 as of 8:30 am, pointing to a gap-down opening for the Nifty 50, which had closed at 24,378.1 in the previous session.

Brent crude crosses $100 as Iran tensions rise, peace talks stallOil prices have now risen for a fourth straight session, supported by larger-than-expected draws in US gasoline and distillate inventories, indicating robust demand.

Gold at Rs 1.52 lakh, silver near Rs 2.47 lakh: Should you stay invested or sell?Gold and silver prices are moving sharply, and many investors are wondering what to do next. With gold around Rs 1.52 lakh and silver near Rs 2.47 lakh, is it time to stay invested or consider selling?
Govt allows ethanol blending in Aviation Turbine FuelThe move is aimed at promoting Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) and reducing India's dependence on imported oil.
Why more roads won't fix Gurugram's traffic crisisGurugram's population has doubled from 1.5 million in 2011 to over 3 million today. Only 12 to 15% of residents regularly use public transport.
Sensex ends 757 points lower, Nifty below 24,400; HCLTech sinks 11%The S&P BSE Sensex tanked 756.84 points to close at 78,516.49, while the NSE Nifty50 lost 198.50 points to end at 24,378.10.

entertainment.headline: Will Dhruva Sarja's KD give Kannada cinema its much-needed push in 2026? Dhruva Sarja's KD is set to hit theatres on April 30, raising hopes for a strong box office revival for Kannada cinema.

Learnt it the hard way: Kritika Kamra on hiding relationship with Gaurav Kapur Actor Kritika Kamra said keeping her relationship with cricket commentator Gaurav Kapur private was absolutely intentional as she waited to be sure. She said she did not fully understand the impact of putting everything out in the open when she was younger.

Kajol to Gen Z amid information overload: Make up your mind Actor Kajol said Gen Z faces difficulty in deciding what they want due to constant information overload. She added that knowing what is right and wrong is not the same as making personal choices.

Ayushmann, Aparshakti's Roop Di Rani reel is 90s throwback: Life comes full circle Actor Ayushmann Khurrana shared a throwback dance reel with his actor-brother Aparshakti Khurrana. He linked Pati Patni Aur Woh Do song Roop Di Rani to a childhood memory. The film is set to release in theatres on May 15.

Arijit Singh, wife Koel Roy arrive hand in hand to vote in Murshidabad. Watch A viral video showed singer Arijit Singh walking hand in hand with his wife, Koel Roy, as he cast his vote in Murshidabad. Another clip of the singer at the polling booth was also widely shared online.

Kartik Aaryan-Sreeleela film on track for release this year, says Anurag Basu Director Anurag Basu cleared the air on the shooting timeline of his romantic film starring Kartik Aaryan and Sreeleela. He said that the film is on schedule and progressing well.

Dhurandhar 2 ending gets an AI twist with Hamza and Yalina. Seen it yet? An AI version of Dhurandhar 2 ending has caught widespread attention. The reimagined clip gives a unique twist to Ranveer Singh and Sara Arjun's characters, Hamza and Yalina, and their relationship.

Is Karan Wahi quitting acting amid spiritual journey? Actor reacts Karan Wahi has reacted to rumours of quitting acting following his spiritual remarks that went viral on social media. He clarified that his growing interest in spirituality will not affect his acting career.

I will always release my films in theatres first: Anubhav Sinha Director Anubhav Sinha has made a popular OTT series as IC814: The Kandahar Hijack, but the maker of films such as Article 15, Thappad, Mulk and Assi says his first love will always be the big screen.

Jackky Bhagnani says marriage to Rakul Preet is situationship but exclusive Actor-producer Jackky Bhagnani said his marriage to actor Rakul Preet Singh is like a situationship. The confession came in a recent interview, where the Bollywood couple opened up about their relationship dynamics. Both highlighted a bond where neither depends on the other for happiness.

Akshay Kumar, Vidya Balan reunite: All you need to know about their fourth film Actors Akshay Kumar and Vidya Balan have reunited for a comedy-drama film. Akshay shared a video of them leaving together for Kerala and confirmed their fourth project.

Krithi Shetty joins Anil Ravipudi's next, Keerthy Suresh likely opposite Venkatesh Director Anil Ravipudi is set to bring Keerthy Suresh and Krithi Shetty on board for his next film with Venkatesh and Kalyanram.

Fan gifts Quran to Anne Hathaway at London event after viral Inshallah clip. Watch Anne Hathaway grabbed attention at The Devil Wears Prada 2 London premiere after she accepted a Quran from a fan. This came after the viral buzz around her 'Inshallah' clip.

Mercy review: Thought-provoking, but not quite immersiveRelease Date:Thu Apr 23, 2026 Mercy review: The film takes on the difficult subject of passive euthanasia with sincerity, but struggles to translate its intent into a consistently engaging cinematic experience.
Imran Khan denies working on Ashneer Grover biopic Actor Imran Khan reacted to rumours about starring in Ashneer Grover's biopic. The actor said he had not even heard of the project.

Raja Shivaji trailer revives interest in Shivaji's Wagh Nakh. All about the weapon The Raja Shivaji trailer brings the Wagh Nakh into focus once more. Riteish Deshmukh as Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj wields the iconic weapon, long seen as a symbol of strategy and power.

Governor first poster out: Manoj Bajpayee fronts a tense nation-in-crisis tale Manoj Bajpayee's new film Governor: The Silent Saviour, was announced on his 57th birthday. The film is directed by Chinmay Mandlekar.

Prime Video, ZEE5, JioHotstar: 24, Marty Supreme, Pratichaya and more Prime Video, ZEE5 and JioHotstar have an exciting lineup of releases this week across genres and languages. From Marty Supreme and Pratichaya to 24, here's what you can stream.

All about Ramayana's grand opening scene: Ravana-Kubera's epic battle Ramayana is set to begin on an unexpected note, shifting focus away from the familiar Rama–Ravana face-off. Instead, the film's opening draws from a lesser-known episode that could redefine how audiences see Ravana's rise and ambition.

general news.headline: India slams Trump's 'hellhole' jibe, says comments uninformed, inappropriateIndia criticised US President Donald Trump for amplifying a post that referred to India as a "hellhole on the planet," calling the remarks uninformed, inappropriate, in poor taste and not reflective of the broader bilateral relationship.

What highest-ever voting of 92% in West Bengal election revealsWest Bengal saw a 92.59% voter turnout, its highest ever, in Phase 1 of the Assembly election. What does the high turnout, which follows the SIR exercise, reveal about the ground situation?

Had didi given money...: Ex-help remorseless after killing IRS officer's daughterDuring questioning, Rahul Meena, 23, maintained a calm and composed demeanour, and stressed that he had only gone to the IRS officer's home in Delhi's upscale Kailash Hills to get money.

Non-application of mind: Congress on EC notice over Kharge's 'terrorist' remarkMallikarjun Kharge, during a campaign event in Tamil Nadu, called PM Narendra Modi a 'terrorist' while criticising AIADMK's alliance with BJP in Tamil Nadu. The Congress chief, however, clarified his statement, saying that he meant BJP's intimidation tactics through central agencies.

No slots, no 10-minute help: As maids head to Bengal, apps struggle to keep upWith many workers travelling to West Bengal for the polls, households are increasingly turning to apps like Urban Company, Snabbit, and Pronto, where high demand is tightening availability during peak hours.

Highest turnout since 1947: Bengal sees 92.25% polling, Tamil Nadu 85.03%Polling has concluded in both states, covering 152 of West Bengal's 294 constituencies in the first phase and all 234 seats in Tamil Nadu, which went to the polls in a single-day contest. Both states are witnessing high-stakes electoral battles, with outcomes expected to significantly shape their political landscape.

Live: Iran activates air defences, engages 'hostile targets', local media reportsUS-Iran War Live Updates: Senior Iranian officials have accused Washington of stalling peace negotiations, citing a US naval blockade of Iranian ports as a key obstacle, while Iran's Revolutionary Guard said it seized two foreign vessels in the Strait of Hormuz and fired on a third for alleged maritime violations. President Masoud Pezeshkian said Tehran remains open to "dialogue and agreement" but that breaches of commitments, blockades and threats are hindering meaningful talks. US President Donald Trump has signalled he remains prepared to resume military action if negotiations fail. Meanwhile, Lebanon condemned Israel over the killing of journalist Amal Khalil and the wounding of her colleague Zeinab Faraj, accusing it of violating a ceasefire through continued strikes and demolitions. Catch all the live updates at indiatoday.in.

Join a gym, you'll be in better shape: TCS Nashik survivor recalls ordealThe woman, among the nine complainants in the TCS Nashik forced conversion and sex harassment case, alleged that the accused would call her to their desks and stare at her inappropriately, make personal comments and bodyshame her.
How do Earth and Moon appear from Mars? See Nasa picNasa's HiRISE camera aboard the Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter captured Earth and its Moon from 142 million kilometres away.

Tough nut to crack: US as India seeks edge over rivals in trade talksThe fresh round of trade talks come amid wider uncertainty over Trump's tariffs, which has complicated efforts to clinch a deal. India is pushing for preferential access to the US market, while Washington is seeking greater openings in agriculture and related sectors.

Indian seafarer among crew of ship captured by Iran, govt says it's worriedOne Indian seafarer was among the crew of a vessel detained by Iran in the Strait of Hormuz, prompting concern from the government as maritime tensions intensify and ships in the region come under fire. All Indian seafarers are reported safe.

Gen Naravane reveals meaning of 'jo uchit samjho woh karo' remark in his bookThe controversy erupted in February when Rahul Gandhi attempted to quote from General Naravane's unpublished memoir. The Congress MP said the alleged remark by PM Modi, cited in the book, during the border dispute with China suggested abdication of responsibility.

Non-resident Bengali's fiery post fuels debate on Bengal's economy, jobs amid pollsBitan Chakraborty, an assistant professor of finance in Delhi-NCR, said he was born and brought up in Delhi and had only spent extended time in Kolkata for two years during his MBA, apart from annual summer visits.

Hours after Trump's hellhole remark against India, US's damage controlAfter a controversy erupted over his remarks, President Donald Trump spoke warmly of India, calling it "a great country" and a close partner, according to a US embassy spokesperson.

India advises its nationals not to travel to Iran as flights resumeIn the advisory, the Ministry of External Affairs mentioned that regional uncertainties could impact flight operations, leading to potential safety concerns for Indian nationals.
No relief: Heatwaves to get worse in North India, temp to climb on FridayTemperatures across northwest and central India are likely to climb a further 2–3C before any relief arrives.

Jackky Bhagnani says marriage to Rakul Preet is situationship but exclusiveActor-producer Jackky Bhagnani said his marriage to actor Rakul Preet Singh is like a situationship. The confession came in a recent interview, where the Bollywood couple opened up about their relationship dynamics. Both highlighted a bond where neither depends on the other for happiness.

Video: How masked Iranian commandos seized India-bound ship in HormuzA day after two merchant vessels were seized, including one bound for Gujarat, Iran released dramatic footage showing masked commandos taking control of the ships.

Ex-help raped Alwar woman 12 hours before raping and killing IRS officer's daughterA 23-year-old former house help allegedly carried out two rapes within 12 hours -- first in Rajasthan's Alwar and then in a Delhi apartment belonging to his former employer, the police said.

Ground Zero Murshidabad: Did ballots silence bullets this time?The attack on Humayun Kabir was a stark reminder of Murshidabad's bloody legacy, though the scale of violence felt contained compared to past polls. What happened in Nowda seemed like an aberration on an otherwise heavily guarded day.

Planned for 10 years, fled in 2 hours: How family of 9 escaped North Korea by seaA family's audacious plan for freedom from a dictatorial North Korea took a decade of preparation before they crossed the Yellow Sea into South Korea's waters. From learning to fish to hiding in plain sight, nine members of the Kim family escaped from North Korea on a stormy night. Here's how.

Did Iran's power struggle stall planned peace talks with US in Islamabad?Internal disagreements within Iran's leadership have reportedly delayed plans to dispatch a negotiating team to Islamabad for talks with the US on ending the eight-week-old conflict, fuelling renewed debate over whose decision-making power truly rests in Tehran.

Is Iran emerging as the world's fourth superpower?Iran has emerged stronger since the war initiated by the US and Israel. In the Strait of Hormuz, Tehran has found a new weapon. Though no match to the US, China and Russia economically, Iran has shown resilience and a chokehold. Is Iran emerging as the world's fourth superpower?

Tamil Nadu sees highest-ever voter turnout, breaks 2011 recordPolling concluded in Tamil Nadu at 6 pm across all 234 constituencies, with those in queues permitted to vote. Voter turnout reached 85.03 per cent, surpassing the 2011 Assembly elections' record.

sports.headline: CSK stand with Mukesh Choudhary, dedicate record win vs MI to pacer after personal loss IPL 2026, MI vs CSK: Fast bowler Mukesh Choudhary played through personal grief as CSK dedicated their record 103-run win over Mumbai Indians to him after the passing of his mother.

AI vs Human: Sony's table tennis robot 'Ace' outplays national-level players A robot built by Sony has defeated elite human table tennis players in a breakthrough that researchers say could mark a new phase in robotics, particularly in tasks that demand speed, precision and real-time decision-making.

Samson, spin twins decimate MI as CSK win IPL El Clasico without Thala IPL, MI vs CSK: Chennai Super Kings inflicted Mumbai Indians' biggest defeat by runs in IPL history, cruising to a dominant El Clásico win on the back of Sanju Samson's unbeaten century and Akeal Hosein's match-turning spell.

Sanju turns up vs arch-rivals, becomes CSK's joint-top centurion in just 7 games MI vs CSK: Sanju Samson silenced the Wankhede crowd with a masterclass in resilience and power-hitting. In his first appearance for CSK against the arch-rivals, Samson slammed a century to join Suresh Raina and Shane Watson in an elite bracket.

Away from IPL, Mustafizur Rahman emulates Wasim Akram to rock New Zealand in ODI series BAN vs NZ, 3rd ODI: Mustafizur Rahman finished with figures of 9-2-43-5 as Bangladesh beat New Zealand by 55 runs in Chattogram on Thursday to win the three-match series 2-1.

Surplus spinner? Delhi Capitals baffle fans with signing of Ben Duckett replacement IPL 2026: Delhi Capitals have signed England spinner Rehan Ahmed as a replacement for Ben Duckett for the remainder of the tournament. While Rehan has batting credentials, his speciality lies in bowling leg-spin.

Mukesh Choudhary turns up for CSK after mother's passing, team pays tribute MI vs CSK: Mukesh Choudhary returns for CSK days after his mother's passing, as teammates wear black armbands in tribute during the MI clash at Wankhede.

What makes Shreyas Iyer an effective captain? PBKS pacer Xavier Bartlett explains Xavier Bartlett hailed Shreyas Iyer's calm leadership, sharp game awareness, and ability to back his bowlers, crediting him for PBKS' strong run in IPL 2026. Beyond PBKS, Iyer has a proven captaincy record, having led Delhi Capitals and KKR in previous seasons.

MI vs CSK without Dhoni, Rohit dampens El Clasico buzz Fans were left disappointed as two of the Indian Premier League's biggest names, MS Dhoni and Rohit Sharma, missed the IPL 2026 El Clasico between the Mumbai Indians and the Chennai Super Kings at the Wankhede Stadium on April 23.

Tempers flare in PSL as Mohammad Amir, Faheem Ashraf trade heated words in ugly spat Tempers flared in the Pakistan Super League clash as Mohammad Amir and Faheem Ashraf engaged in a heated on-field exchange after a key dismissal. Amir's fiery send-off triggered Ashraf's reaction, leading to a brief confrontation before players intervened.

Pakistan's Nawaz breaks silence on doping allegations, submits stance to ICC Experienced Pakistan all-rounder Mohammad Nawaz has submitted his response to the International Cricket Council and the Pakistan Cricket Board after testing positive during the T20 World Cup, co-hosted by India and Sri Lanka earlier this year, insisting that he did not knowingly ingest any banned substance.

Lamine Yamal's FIFA World Cup hopes still intact after hamstring injuryLamine Yamal's hopes to take part in the upcoming FIFA World Cup remain intact after FC Barcelona confirmed that he is expected to be available for the tournament.

IPL: Rohit Sharma should be used as an impact substitute, says Sanjay BangarSanjay Bangar reckons MI can use Rohit Sharma as an impact sub vs CSK despite injury concerns, highlighting strong form, key player performances, and team balance ahead of the high-stakes clash.

MI vs CSK Highlights: Mumbai suffer complete humiliation, lose by 103 runsMumbai (MI) vs Chennai (CSK) Highlights, IPL 2026: Mumbai suffer their worst defeat in the history of the tournament as Chennai complete an out and out demolition at the Wankhede.

IPL: Akash Madhwal joins Chennai Super Kings as injured Ayush Mhatre's replacementChennai Super Kings have signed Akash Madhwal as a replacement for Ayush Mhatre, who has been ruled out of the remainder of the Indian Premier League 2026 due to injury.

RCB's Nuwan Thushara withdraws case against Sri Lanka Cricket over IPL 2026 NOCFast bowler Nuwan Thushara, who had dragged Sri Lanka Cricket to court for not issuing him a No Objection Certificate (NoC) to compete in the Indian Premier League, on Thursday withdrew the case against the governing body.

I'm sorry, boss: Ex-India selector apologises to Rohit for 2011 World Cup snubKris Srikkanth said he apologised to Rohit Sharma for leaving him out of India's 2011 ICC Cricket World Cup squad. He explained that the selectors chose an all-rounder-heavy team inspired by the 1983 Cricket World Cup model.

Stay in India Bethell, Cook knows nothing of IPL: Pietersen fires at ex-teammateIPL 2026: Kevin Pietersen has defended Jacob Bethell's IPL stint, taking aim at Alastair Cook's criticism. The former England captains remain divided on whether learning in the IPL outweighs the need for regular match time.

Premier League weekend preview: Arsenal, Spurs and Man United eye crucial winsArsenal, Tottenham Hotspur and Manchester United head into crucial Premier League clashes with pressure mounting, as injuries, form and league positions shape key encounters.

Bottle-job in sight for Arsenal? Manchester City back on top after Burnley winManchester City seized the Premier League lead from Arsenal following a narrow 1-0 victory over Burnley, marking the first time they have topped the table since the opening week.

Mayank Yadav criticism horrifying: Bishop defends LSG pacer's costly IPL returnIPL 2026: Ian Bishop has backed Mayank Yadav after criticism over his IPL return. The former pacer called the backlash "horrifying" and urged fans to focus on the youngster's recovery and long-term fitness.

Mitchell Starc cleared to play IPL: DC pacer expected to be available for RR clashIPL 2026: Mitchell Starc has been cleared to return to IPL 2026 after recovering from injury. The Delhi Capitals pacer is set to rejoin the squad and could feature in their upcoming clash against Rajasthan Royals.

India eye strong Thomas & Uber Cup run as badminton heads to DenmarkIndia head into the Thomas & Uber Cup 2026 with a mix of proven stars and rising talent. With Ayush Shetty's recent surge and Sindhu leading the women, the focus will be on how far these squads can go.

IPL's war against left-arm spin and what Ravindra Jadeja makes of itIPL 2026: The IPL's reliance on match-ups has reshaped how left-arm spinners are used, often at the cost of context. Ravindra Jadeja weighs in on the balance between data, conditions, and on-field judgement.

Cartoons first, bowlers next: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi reveals his IPL routine at 15IPL 2026: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi continues to grab attention with his performances and personality in IPL 2026. The 15-year-old's revelation about watching cartoons before matches adds a unique layer to his growing story.

Rajasthan Royals' Nandre Burger fined for abusive send off to LSG's Rishabh PantIPL 2026: Nandre Burger, pacer for Rajasthan Royals, was fined for breaching the Indian Premier League Code of Conduct after an abusive send-off to Rishabh Pant. He was also handed one demerit point for his behaviour.

Donald Trump envoy pushes FIFA to replace Iran with Italy at World Cup 2026FIFA World Cup 2026 faces fresh controversy after a proposal to replace Iran with Italy from none other than USA President Donald Trump's envoy. While the suggestion has sparked debate, FIFA has not indicated any change to the current tournament lineup.

Lamine Yamal's World Cup 2026 hopes in doubt after FC Barcelona injury scareLamine Yamal suffered an injury scare during Barcelona's La Liga clash, raising concerns ahead of a crucial phase. The young star now faces a race against time to recover before the FIFA World Cup 2026.

MI vs CSK: Will MS Dhoni spice up strange IPL El Clasico of fallen giants?IPL 2026, MI vs CSK: Mumbai Indians and Chennai Super Kings face off in IPL 2026's first El Clasico on Thursday, but this time the contest carries a different edge, with both five-time champions still searching for consistency. The spotlight, as ever, is on MS Dhoni, who is on the verge of a return after a calf strain, while Rohit Sharma is also pushing to come back from a hamstring injury.

IPL 2026 Play of the Day: How Rishabh Pant's stupid swipe sank Lucknow at homeIPL 2026, LSG vs RR: Rishabh Pant lasted just three balls, but that brief stay set the tone for a muddled batting effort from Lucknow Super Giants. Chasing 160, they squandered the hard work of their fast bowlers and never truly looked in control against Rajasthan Royals.

Laura Wolvaardt's South Africa outclass India to take 3-0 series leadSouth Africa secured an unassailable 3-0 lead in the five-match T20I series with a commanding nine-wicket win over India in the third match at the Wanderers Stadium in Johannesburg on Wednesday.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
